design_philosophy.core_loop: Infiltrate enemy installations using character-specific skills, avoid detection via surveillance systems, solve kidnapping case across multiple locations (Venice to Swiss Alps)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Stealth vs. action balance: discretion advised during infiltration to avoid triggering security, but civilians cannot be harmed on streets without mission failure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: IMF operative conducting covert espionage missions with a specialized team, controlling multiple agents with distinct capabilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Metal Gear-inspired stealth-action hybrid leaning more toward action than pure stealth; enforces moral constraint (no civilian casualties) that creates unique failure state distinct from combat failure

difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based progression system for continuing between stages
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Regional difficulty balancing: North American version features 2-3x damage scaling on enemies and environmental traps compared to European versions, suggesting developer tuned harder experience for NA market. Invulnerability cheat available in US version only (LRHN password + controller input combination)

progression.upgrade_system: Character switching mechanic—three IMF operatives (Max, Grant, Nicholas) with different skills required for completion
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression via password system; character abilities unlock by selecting appropriate operative for mission requirements
replayability_hooks: Multiple characters with distinct skill sets encourage different playstyle approaches
Password system enables non-linear stage access for speedrunning or challenge runs

world_building_techniques: Mission briefing setup (kidnapping case, Sinister 7 antagonist)
Geographic progression signifies narrative advancement (Venice → Swiss Alps)
Environmental variety (multiple-floor installations, urban streets, alpine bases) supports spy fiction setting
TV series IP licensing provides pre-existing lore foundation

notable_bosses: Slash Stiletto (appears at end of first stage, grey variant throws bombs)
Fido T. Flamethrower (enemy type throwing Molotov cocktails—6 damage NA / 2 damage EU)
Jorgé Burns (flame-thrower enemy in final stage)
Pistol Pete Piranovich (purple enemy, 2 damage bullets NA / 1 damage EU)

faction_indicators: Local authorities as mission failure trigger (arrest on civilian harm)
danger_signals: Surveillance system detection triggers security guard response
Walking knight variants deal escalating damage across regions
environmental_storytelling: Street civilians establish non-hostile zones contrasting with combat areas
Geographic settings (Venice canals, Swiss Alps, Moscow, Berlin, Cyprus) establish spy thriller atmosphere

sources_used: tcrf - Mission: Impossible (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
